bounded_excerpt: Version date : 2021 Nov 22Work -up should include : History & clinical exam Blood work (serum β-hCG, CBC w /differential , platelet determinations , clotting function tests , liver function tests , & renal function studies ) Imaging to check for metastases (chest x -ray w /CT scan of chest if x -ray is negative , CT of abdomen /pelvis . If there are pulmonary metastases , requires a CT or preferably an MRI of the brain ).Referral to a Gynecologic Oncologist Algorithm for the Management of Gestational Trophoblastic Neoplasia (GYNE -008) Recommended follow -up after evacuation of a molar pregnancy should include : - Weekly quantitative hCG until negative x 3, then monthly x 6 months - Pelvic exams at 1 month post -evacuation - Contraception x 6 months (preferably OCP ) Diagnosis of Gestational Trophoblastic NeoplasiaPatient presents to their family physician /gynecologist with any of the following symptoms following evacuation of a molar pregnancy : (1) Abnormal β-hCG patterns /levels (2) Metastases in addition to abnormal β-hCG levels (3) A histologic diagnosis of choriocarcinoma , placental site trophoblastic tumour , or epithelioid trophoblastic tumour Non-metastatic (Stage I ) an

bounded_excerpt: Version date : 2021 Nov 22Algorithm for the Management of Non -metastatic & Low Risk Gestational Trophoblastic Neoplasia (GYNE -008) Non-metastatic (Stage I ) and low risk metastatic Actinomycin -D & methotrexate w /folinic acid Actinomycin -D* (preferred to methotrexate ) Methotrexate Regimens Non metastatic /low-risk metastatic chemotherapy (CT): Preferred chemotherapy regimens : - Actinomycin -D (1.25 mg/m2 IV ) q2weeks x 1-3 cycles beyond negative β- hCG - Actinomycin -D (0.5 mg/m2 IV given 1-2 days) & methotrexate (100mg/m2 IV push + 300mg/m2 IV on day 1) w/folinic acid (15 mg PO , q6h x 9 doses starting 24 hrs after methotrexate bolus ), q2wks x 1-3 cycles beyond negative β-hCG - Methotrexate (50 mg/m2 IM) weekly x 1-3 cycles beyond negative β-hCG Other regimens : Methotrexate (50mg/m2 IM, days 1,3,5,7) & folinic acid (7.5 mg PO , days 2,4,6,8), q2wks x 1-3 cycles beyond negative β-hCG Methotrexate (100 mg/m2 IV) and folinic acid (15mg PO , q6h x 4 doses starting 24hrs after methotrexate ), given weekly x 1-3 cycles beyond negative β-hCGPreferred Chemotherapy RegimensOR OR OR Follow -up and Surveillance Serial serum β-hCG measurements : Weekly until negative x 3, then monthly

bounded_excerpt: Version date : 2021 Nov 22Algorithm for the Management of High Risk Metastatic Gestational Trophoblastic Neoplasia (GYNE -008) Preferred Chemotherapy Regimens EMA /CO multi agent CT EMA /EP multi agent CT MACE multi agent CT RT in select ptsConsider surgery for resection of metastases Regimens High -risk metastatic chemotherapy (CT): Preferred chemotherapy regimens : - EMA /CO* multi agent chemotherapy q 2 wks for 3 cycles beyond negative β- hCG Etoposide (100mg/m2 IV, days 1,2) Actinomycin -D (0.5 mg IV push days 1,2) Methotrexate (300 mg/m2 IV, day 1) Folinic acid (15mg PO , q 12 h, 4 doses starting 24 hours after methotrexate Vincristine (0.8-1.0 mg/m2 IV, day 8) Cyclophosphamide (600 mg/m2 IV, day 8) Consider GCS -f 5 mcg/kg on days 4-6(&) and 10-12(13) of each cycle - EMA /EP* multi agent chemotherapy , given every 2 weeks for 3 cycles beyond negative β-hCG Course 1: same as EMA /CO Course 2: etoposide (150 mg/m2 IV, day 8), cisplatin (75 mg/m2 IV, day 8), plus magnesium supplementation (30 ml PO , q12h, day 1) and Fligastrium (GCS -f 5 mcg/kg SC on days 9-14) *Consider low -dose induction therapy with EP etoposide (100 mg/m2 IV on day 1 & 2 every 7 days for 1-3 courses ) prio
